original_behavior: scripts/ingest-source-v1.mjs used YouTube Data API with a broad exact tool-name query q=tool.name. scripts/ingest-evidence.mjs also generated SearXNG web queries: site:youtube.com "tool" tutorial, review, workflow, plus "tool" youtube with exclusions and alias expansion.
current_behavior: scripts/rebuild-proof-sources-v1.mjs uses one YouTube Data API query per tool: "{tool} tutorial workflow review comparison demo" with videoDuration=medium and maxResults=10.
difference: Reduced query breadth and collapsed many intents into one AND-like phrase. Removed SearXNG web discovery, aliases, and multiple separate YouTube query variants.
estimated_coverage_impact: High negative: fewer candidate videos are discovered, especially when creators use only one intent term like tutorial or review.
estimated_quality_impact: Mixed: query is higher-intent, but may miss strong videos that do not contain the full combined phrase.
recommendation: Restore separate high-intent YouTube query variants and alias expansion; keep stricter quality filtering after discovery.

original_behavior: Original source ingestion returned top videos for tool.name up to CLI limit; SearXNG evidence path could discover YouTube pages through public search and snippets.
current_behavior: Only direct YouTube API search is used; no SearXNG/public web results, no cached historical candidates, no article/search-derived YouTube links.
difference: Removed public search discovery layer and historical candidate pool.
estimated_quality_impact: Potentially positive if API results are cleaner, but lower discovery volume can reduce top-three quality.
recommendation: Use YouTube API plus public search result candidates; dedupe and rank after merging.

original_behavior: Original source ingestion normalized videos with no strong intent filter. Earlier evidence feed stored many records and later display selected summaries.
current_behavior: Rejects official domains, Shorts title hints, low-intent terms, unrelated mentions, and requires at least one intent term in title/description/channel.
difference: Filtering is much stricter and selection stores max 3 items per tool/source.
estimated_quality_impact: Positive: fewer weak/generic records and no empty shells.
recommendation: Keep strict selection, but broaden discovery before filtering.

original_behavior: scripts/ingest-source-v1.mjs used GitHub API query: "{tool} in:name,description,readme" sorted by stars. scripts/ingest-evidence.mjs also generated site:github.com "tool" and "tool" github public-search queries with aliases.
current_behavior: scripts/rebuild-proof-sources-v1.mjs uses three narrower GitHub API queries: "{tool} sdk api", "{tool} integration example", and "{tool} template wrapper implementation" in:name,description,readme.
difference: Narrower queries likely require extra intent words alongside the tool name. Removed broad tool-name repo search and public site:github.com discovery.
estimated_quality_impact: Positive but overly strict: high trust, low recall.
recommendation: Restore broad GitHub repo search as candidate discovery, then score for SDK/API/integration/template/wrapper evidence.

original_behavior: Original GitHub ingestion accepted top repositories by stars and included repo metadata: description, stars, owner, created_at. Public-search route could find GitHub pages beyond repository API search.
current_behavior: Only repository API search is used and only repos matching narrow intent terms are candidates. No README fetch, code search, topics, issues, package/import detection, or public-search fallback.
difference: Removed broad candidate pool and did not add deeper code/README verification to compensate.
estimated_quality_impact: Mixed: fewer false positives, but many real implementations are missed.
recommendation: Two-stage pipeline: broad repo search first, optional README/code metadata scoring second, store only top 3.

original_behavior: Original source ingestion deduped by tool/source/url and confidence; ranking was mostly GitHub stars from API sort.
current_behavior: Rejects forks, archived repos, official URLs, unrelated text, low-intent/listicle text; ranks by intent terms, recent pushed_at, and stars.
difference: Ranking is more quality-oriented but filter requires intent terms in limited title/description data only.
estimated_quality_impact: Positive for stored records, but may reject real usage without intent keywords.
recommendation: Keep rank factors but score them after a broader candidate fetch; avoid requiring intent terms in only title/description.

original_behavior: scripts/ingest-evidence.mjs discovered articles via SearXNG public web search from tool queries: "tool" tutorial/review/workflow/use case with exclusions. scripts/ingest-source-v1.mjs also collected RSS from The Rundown, Ben's Bites, Latent Space, FutureTools, OpenAI Blog, Anthropic Blog, and Vercel Blog.
current_behavior: scripts/rebuild-proof-sources-v1.mjs uses only a fixed RSS pool: Latent Space, The Rundown, Ben's Bites, FutureTools, VentureBeat AI, The Verge AI, TechCrunch AI, and ZDNet AI. No web search.
difference: Removed SearXNG public article discovery and limited articles to recent/feed-visible RSS items.
estimated_quality_impact: Positive for trusted domains, but many legitimate guides/reviews are excluded.
recommendation: Restore public web search discovery for trusted articles while keeping publisher/domain quality filters.

original_behavior: Original SearXNG path generated separate query variants for tutorial, review, workflow, use case, github, youtube and supported aliases. Matching accepted search-result title/snippet/url mention of tool.
current_behavior: No per-tool article search queries are generated. Matching is only against RSS title/description/link and requires intent terms in the same text.
difference: Removed per-tool article query generation and alias expansion.
estimated_quality_impact: Mixed: strong reduction of SEO spam, but strong articles outside the RSS window disappear.
recommendation: Use SearXNG queries for article candidates, then reject official press releases, SEO farms, listicles, and low-quality domains.

original_behavior: Earlier evidence ingestion classified any non-X/non-YouTube/non-GitHub/non-directory as article, later source classification separated docs/news/newsletter/official/other. It stored many search result snippets before strict article proof rules existed.
current_behavior: Requires trusted RSS source, direct tool mention, intent term, non-official URL, and max 3 items per source/tool.
difference: Much stricter source and intent threshold plus no large candidate list.
estimated_quality_impact: Positive, but trust threshold may be too narrow.
recommendation: Keep max-3 selection and no empty shells; broaden candidate retrieval with trusted web search.
